forecast-period.text: A trough over far western SA linking to a weak front south of the Bight will continue to move over western and southern parts of SA during today. A high pressure will move south of the Bight early in the week, before another trough and upper level system develops in the west and moves across mid to late in the week, with a vigorous cold front to follow later Thursday and into Friday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Northwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southwesterly in the late afternoon.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.
South to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots decreasing to about 10 knots in the morning then becoming east to southeasterly in the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Cloudy.
East to northeasterly below 10 knots tending north to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning west to southwesterly in the afternoon. Winds reaching up to 20 knots offshore in the evening.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres.
Mostly sunny.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning south to southeasterly below 10 knots in the late afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
Cloudy.
Easterly below 10 knots tending northeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the morning then tending northerly during the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning west to southwesterly in the evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Beachport during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres.
Southeasterly around 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots south of Port MacDonnell early in the morning. Winds becoming variable below 10 knots in the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Cloudy.
Variable about 10 knots becoming north to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
